meta_title: For Students: debugging with AI to Solve slow development speed (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome slow development speed. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: Accelerate Your Development Workflow
content.seo_description: Master AI-assisted debugging to eliminate slow development cycles. An advanced guide for students using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship faster.
content.answer_block: Stop staring at stack traces for hours. By integrating AI-native agents like Antigravity and Claude Code into your local environment, you automate root-cause analysis. These tools parse complex dependency chains and suggest syntactical fixes in real-time, effectively turning your debugging bottleneck into a streamlined, automated validation pipeline.
content.intro:
You are staring at a terminal flooded with red text, the silence of your workspace heavy with the frustration of a stalled build. Hours of 'print' debugging have yielded nothing but a deeper sense of imposter syndrome. The gap between your vision and the actualized code feels like a chasm that only senior engineers can cross.

But the rules of the game have changed. You no longer need to manually trace every callback or memory leak. By leveraging AI-first tooling, you can offload the cognitive burden of debugging to agents that 'understand' your entire codebase context, turning those hours of struggle into minutes of strategic refinement.

steps.title: Contextualize the Repository
steps.description: Before debugging, the AI needs a mental map of your project architecture to avoid hallucinations.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this directory structure and generate a dependency map. Identify potential circular dependencies and high-risk modules.

steps.title: Isolate the Failure Point
steps.description: Feed the stack trace to the AI while providing the specific component scope to prevent broad, irrelevant suggestions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Examine the following stack trace: [INSERT STACK TRACE]. Locate the exact function call causing the exception in our current codebase.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'find the root cause of the error in src/auth.ts'

steps.title: Automated Hypothesis Generation
steps.description: Ask the AI to propose three distinct solutions ranging from quick-fix to architectural refactoring.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Propose 3 solutions for this bug: 1) A hotfix, 2) A refactor for better readability, 3) A performance-optimized approach.

steps.title: Safe-Mode Implementation
steps.description: Use the AI to write a regression test before applying the fix to ensure the bug doesn't return.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test case that recreates this specific bug. Then provide the patch that makes the test pass.

steps.title: Codebase-Wide Impact Analysis
steps.description: Check if your fix impacts downstream dependencies or unexpected modules across the repo.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Run a static analysis on the proposed change. Will this update break any imports in the /components directory?

pro_tips: Always provide the AI with your 'Tech Stack Manifest' (package.json or requirements.txt) at the start of every session to ensure library-specific accuracy.
Use 'Chain-of-Thought' prompting: ask the AI to explain its reasoning before it writes a single line of code.
If the AI suggests a major refactor, ask it to 'provide a migration path' to avoid breaking existing features.
Treat the AI as a junior dev: review every line of suggested code as if you were doing a peer review in a real engineering team.
Use Claude Code's memory feature to store 'project norms'—such as your preference for functional programming or specific naming conventions—to keep output consistent.
content.next_steps: Once you have successfully patched your bug, commit the changes, write a brief post-mortem in your project README, and move to optimizing your test suite using the same AI-driven workflow.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start mastering your craft. By automating the mundane, you free up your brain for the high-level architecture that actually matters. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
